programmes.show_description: A man has broken his leg on Scafell Pike. As darkness closes in, can the mountain rescue team save him from potentially life-threatening plummeting temperatures? (Ep2/4)
Max's Bar & Grill: Gordon visits New Jersey to transform Max's Bar & Grill. It's been serving up famous hotdogs for almost 100 years but is close to collapse.
(2020) Comedy-drama with Michelle Pfeiffer. A skint Manhattan socialite moves to Paris with her son and a cat... which may or may not contain the spirit of her dead husband.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Dumfries, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien get to work on Maggie's farm, which boasts an extraordinary collection of memorabilia.
With planning permission granted, Keith and Marj start removing the pews, parquet floor and pulpit in the main hall, and make a discovery in the Sunday school ceiling vents.
It's the semi-final and the potters make mosaics and water fountains inspired by Rome's Trevi Fountain. Whose final-worthy creations will make a splash?
Joining the crew today is Neil Delamere. Speaking of Neils - can GB history-maker Neil Simpson build on the two medals he won at the last Winter Paralympics? The 2026 Games start today!
Frasier brings some of his patients into the bar, and Woody cuts Sam's hair. Another Sam - GB skier Cozens - will be cutting down the slopes at the Winter Paralympics, which start today!
Woody's stuck doing back-to-back shifts to pay for a ring for Kelly. And snowboarder Ollie Hill is hoping to make it back-to-back medals at his second Winter Paralympics, which start today!
Sam tries to get even with John Hill after breaking a tooth. Meanwhile with the Winter Paralympics starting today, what ups and downs are in store as GB hits the Italian hills and slopes?
Robert joins a strange support group. But it's ParalympicsGB who really need our support at the Winter Paralympics, which start today!
Everybody loves Raymond, including his and Debra's therapist. And everybody loves curling too. Can GB's wheelchair curlers make the podium in Italy? The Winter Paralympics start today!
Ray's unhappy with the kids' workload and complains to their teacher. Too bad they don't have actual music teacher - and GB snowboarder - Nina Sparks. The Winter Paralympics start today!
Frasier wants to know who the father of Roz's baby is. Well, despite becoming a dad last year, we know it's not GB snowboarder James Barnes-Miller... The Winter Paralympics start today!
Frasier gets caught up in the hype of his 1000th show. Meanwhile, the Winter Paralympics celebrates its 50th anniversary in Milano Cortina this year - and it all kicks off today!
Frasier agrees to go on a cruise ship to Alaska. Maybe he'd be better off cruising to Italy instead, then he'd have a front-row seat at the Winter Paralympics, which starts today!
Frasier begins a relationship with a dynamic criminal defence lawyer. While in Italy, GB's Neil Simpson aims to defend his Super-G skiing title at the Winter Paralympics, which start today!
Mike and Frank unearth a life-sized Elvis sculpture in rural Vermont. Meanwhile, Channel 4's All Shook Up with Winter Paralympics fever, ahead of the Games' opening ceremony tonight.
Channel 4's midday news summary.
Tim and Fuzz revamp a classic Swedish Saab. Sweden beat GB in the wheelchair curling at the last Winter Paralympics. Can GB get revenge in their clash at the 2026 Games, which start today?!

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon plans to make a dining table from an old seed drill. Will ParalympicsGB's well-drilled athletes make their mark on the medal table at the Winter Paralympics, which start today?
Neil Delamere joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Can GB's Mixed Team wheelchair curlers dent the hopes of favourites China at this year's Winter Paralympics, which start today?
The Flyball's wide open at the world-famous dog show. Meanwhile, will GB snowboarder Davy Zyw be top dog at the Winter Paralympics, which start today? There's no ball, but he'll be flying.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Andrew and Richard are chasing their dream lives in Europe. And do you know who else is chasing their dreams in Europe? That's right, ParalympicsGB. The Winter Paralympics start today!
All the hosts are unhappy with their shocking scores. They're going to wish they were more like ParalympicsGB, who begin their bid to bring home some Winter Paralympics wins today!
In South Wales, Sandra's going heavy on cream and mushrooms. But which athletes will be the crème de la crème of the Winter Paralympics? Which, in case you hadn't guessed, begins today!
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
...Ceremony: Ade Adepitan, Ed Jackson, Dame Sarah Storey and Alice Tai present live as the Milano Cortina Paralympics begin. Which athletes are the ones to watch over the next nine days?
This time the Goggleboxers watch The Apprentice, Britain's Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, and Alexander Armstrong in India.
Comedians Nick Mohammed and Vittorio Angelone and former Conservative politician Penny Mordaunt dissect the week's news with Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker.

programmes.show_name: Rhod Gilbert: The Book of John
programmes.show_description: Raw, personal and brutally honest, award-winning comedian Rhod takes us on a no-nonsense journey into finding humour when you've hit rock-bottom. Recorded at the Wales Millennium Centre.
Bill demonstrates the influence of Cockney music on the classical greats and warns of a world ruled by insects, in his debut live stand-up show at the Bloomsbury Theatre, back in 1996.
Diwan: Gordon helps an Indian restaurant in New York that's been going for 35 years. But a decline in trade has led to fusion experiments - with mixed results.
Fiona and John hope that their guests will embrace their green ethos at Knockaloe Beg Farm on the Isle of Man. Rivals Robbie and John are pleasantly surprised by the cleanliness.
Ex-demolition man Phil Routledge hopes to blow his guests away at the ultra-modern The Arches on the Isle of Man. But some of his high-tech gadgets leave them confused.
Can Robbie and John's traditional Blackpool B&B, The Trafford, impress their well-heeled rivals? The hosts try to sweeten the deal with a lesson in how to make Blackpool rock.
Empty-nester Julie Brogan welcomes her rivals to the grand Sheepy Lodge in Warwickshire, where she faces her worst fear: cooking poached eggs for her guests.
It's the final day of the competition, and the rival B&Bers meet to settle some scores and to find out how much they've been paid - and who's won the week.
Some extra brunch as Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.
With the chapel still uninhabitable, Keith and Marj work on their first on-site bedroom: a shipping container in the car park!
Inner Tube: After playing mud football, Doug dozes off in front of the TV and has some unusual dreams.
Papa Pill: Doug regrets letting Arthur go to a cocktail lounge.
Package Deal: Thinking he's been offered a job at FedEx, Doug tries to renegotiate his package at work. Meanwhile, Carrie overdoes it with the fake tan.
Frinkcoin: Professor Frink develops a cryptocurrency that makes him rich and gets him new friends. But are they true friends?
Todd, Todd, Why Hast Thou Forsaken Me?: Todd loses his faith in God. Homer and Ned share a near-death experience.
Day 1 begins with the Para Alpine Downhill including Neil Simpson, the first British male to win gold on the snow. And will the GB Wheelchair Curling Mixed Team brush Slovakia aside?
Day 1 continues as Scott Meenagh competes in his third and final Paralympics. Plus, after fighting to be fit, can Menna Fitzpatrick make her mark on the Alpine Skiing Downhill?
...Highlights: F1 is back! As well as each other, the drivers must contend with new regulations. Oh, and there's a new team on the grid. But who has the early-season pace in Melbourne?
Radzi Chinyanganya and Clare Balding present all the action, as Claudia Winkleman roves the NEC seeking out the latest hot dog news. Plus: make some noise for the Flyball quarter-finals.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Ade Adepitan rounds up the best of Day 1. Did Menna Fitzpatrick or Neil Simpson achieve a 'piste de résistance' performance in the Downhill Alpine Skiing?
Claudia Winkleman joins Clare Balding for a star-studded night including judging of the Utility and Toy Groups. And it's not strictly ballroom, but it is Heelwork to Music. Plus: Scruffts.
In Ho Chi Minh City, the most populous city in Vietnam, Bill is caught between wartime tunnels, endless crazy traffic, and a deep dive into a local musical twist of metal versus folk. (Ep2)
...Harold Fry: (2023) Rachel Joyce's poignant drama starring Jim Broadbent. A retired man walks from Devon to Berwick to save a former colleague from cancer, using nothing but faith.
